patches.summary: After-call best-effort hooks logging bash/click/screenshot/exec/key actions to the local SQLite ledger powering audit/grep/replay/idle/oversized/cost.
patches.reason: The novel commands (audit, grep, replay, idle, oversized, cost) read a local actions ledger that has no live-API equivalent. The ledger has to be populated from the leaf commands themselves; placing the hook anywhere else would make those novel commands a hollow promise.
patches.validated_outcome: Each leaf logs only after a successful API call; logging errors are silently swallowed via _ = logActionBestEffort(...). Screenshot guards on prov.Source == "live" so cache reads are not double-counted.

patches.summary: extractWorkspaces accepts {"projects":[...]} envelope so fleet sees workspaces returned by the live Orgo API.
patches.reason: The live Orgo API returns {"projects":[...]} (via the /workspaces->/projects rewriter in internal/client/client.go), but the generated fleet command only handled {"workspaces":[...]} and bare-array shapes. Result: fleet reported workspace_count=0 even with valid auth and 13 real workspaces.
patches.validated_outcome: fleet --agent against the real API returns workspace_count=13 with per-workspace computer_count. Issue rollup populates correctly.

patches.summary: filterFieldsRec descends into array-valued fields when no top-level keys match a selector, so --select id,name works against list envelopes like {"projects":[...]}.
patches.reason: Generic PP bug. --select against a wrapped list response returned {} because selector path heads (id, name) never matched the wrapper key (projects). Reasonable user expectation is to apply the selector to each item in the wrapped list. Affects every printed CLI whose list endpoints wrap results in an envelope.
patches.validated_outcome: workspaces list --select id,name --agent returns the projects array with id/name kept per item; bare-array and direct-object selection paths unchanged.

patches.summary: doctor probes /workspaces (an authenticated endpoint) instead of / so 401/403 is a definitive credentials FAIL.
patches.reason: Generic PP bug. doctor probed the API root (/), which on most APIs returns 404 regardless of auth, so an invalid key still rendered Credentials: ok. The new probe path /workspaces (rewritten to /projects by the Orgo client) returns 401 for bad keys and 200 for good keys, giving doctor a definitive verdict.
patches.validated_outcome: Valid key: 'OK Credentials: valid'. Invalid key: 'FAIL Credentials: invalid (HTTP 401 from /workspaces)'.
patches.upstream_issue: TO-FILE: cli-printing-press doctor verify path should be an authenticated endpoint, not /

patches.summary: README Quick Start example for `computers create` uses --workspace-id <workspace-uuid> instead of the non-existent --workspace flag.
patches.reason: Likely a generic README-template bug: the example assumes --workspace, but the generated CLI exposes the OpenAPI parameter name verbatim, which is --workspace-id. Copy-paste of the Quick Start failed.
patches.validated_outcome: Example now copy-pastes successfully against a real workspace ID.
patches.upstream_issue: TO-FILE: cli-printing-press README examples should reflect actual generated flag names
